item.title: All NASA News - NASA
item.description: Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.

item.title: NASA Media Contacts - NASA
item.description: Media Contacts Gerelle DodsonNASA Workforce, STEM Engagement202-358-1600Jennifer DoorenDeputy News Chief, Cybersecurity202-358-1600Alise

item.title: NASA News
item.description: Resources for news media, including NASA news contacts and archived news material

item.title: NASA Names Brian Hughes to Launch Operations Role - NASA
item.description: NASA announced Friday that Brian Hughes will return to the agency as senior director of launch operations, based at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in

item.title: 2026 News Releases - NASA
item.description: Sign up to receive NASA news releases and other information.

item.title: DNA-Inspired Cancer Research; Vision, Heart, and Psychology Tests Wrap Up Week - NASA
item.description: DNA-mimicking materials, quantum physics hardware, and spacesuit batteries wrapped up the work week aboard the International Space Station. The Expedition 74 crew also went into the weekend conducting a series of human research investigations studying the heart, the eyes, and psychology.

item.title: DNA-Inspired Treatments, Space Agriculture Top Crew’s Research Schedule - NASA
item.description: DNA-inspired therapies and space agriculture topped the research schedule aboard the International Space Station on Thursday. The Expedition 74 crew members also serviced a variety of lab hardware including a quantum physics facility, a spacesuit, and life support gear throughout their busy day.

item.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission to Fly by Mars for Gravity Assist
item.description: The spacecraft is using the Red Planet’s gravity to increase speed and tilt its trajectory on the journey to the metal-rich asteroid Psyche.

item.title: NASA Pushes Next-Gen Mars Helicopter Rotor Blades Past Mach 1
item.description: The faster a Mars helicopter’s rotors spin, the heavier the payloads it can transport and the farther it can fly.

item.title: NASA’s Next-Gen Near-Earth Asteroid Space Telescope Takes Shape
item.description: A team of engineers and scientists are building NEO Surveyor to seek out the hardest-to-find asteroids and comets that could impact Earth.

item.title: US-Indian Space Mission Maps Extreme Subsidence in Mexico City
item.description: The NISAR satellite pinpointed areas of the city that are sinking by more than half an inch per month due in large part to groundwater pumping.

item.title: NASA Fires Up Powerful Lithium-Fed Thruster for Trips to Mars
item.description: A novel electromagnetic thruster passed an initial test in a specialized chamber at JPL. With further development, these thrusters could support human missions to the Red Planet.

item.title: NASA’s Perseverance, Curiosity Panoramas Capture Two Sides of Mars
item.description: The two rovers captured scenes that highlight the different journeys they’re taking, helping to piece together the puzzle of the Red Planet’s past.

item.title: NASA’s Curiosity Finds Organic Molecules Never Seen Before on Mars
item.description: Seven organic molecules were identified for the first time on Mars, increasing our understanding of the kinds of molecular preservation possible on the Martian surface.

item.title: NASA Shuts Off Instrument on Voyager 1 to Keep Spacecraft Operating
item.description: To conserve power, engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory needed to turn off one of the science instruments on the most distant spacecraft ever built.

item.title: NASA, SpaceX Launch US-European Satellite to Monitor Earth’s Oceans
item.description: Sentinel-6B will extend a decades-long legacy of global sea level measurements that help forecast weather, protect coastal communities, and more.

item.title: NASA JPL News – Latest Space Science & Missions
item.description: Read official NASA JPL news on Mars rovers, Earth observation, asteroids, exoplanets, and more. Browse 7,500+ stories filtered by topic and date.

item.title: News | Rocket Lab
item.description: Keep up with the latest from Rocket Lab.

item.title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Robotics Leader Motiv Space Systems | Rocket Lab
item.description: The acquisition will add proven robotics technology used in Mars rovers and also insources precision space mechanisms such as solar array drive assemblies, one of the critical components needed for satellite constellation manufacturing.

item.title: Rocket Lab Awarded $30 Million Contract for HASTE Hypersonic Rocket Launches for Anduril | Rocket Lab
item.description: Long Beach, California. May 7, 2026 – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today announced it has been selected by defense technology company Anduril Industries for multiple hypersonic test flights with its HASTE launch vehicle. The…

item.title: Rocket Lab’s Biggest Launch Deal Yet: Confidential Customer Books Multiple Neutron and Electron Launches | Rocket Lab
item.description: The multi-launch contract includes dedicated missions on five Neutron and three Electron rockets for a confidential customer with the launches baselined for 2026-2029

item.title: Rocket Lab and Raytheon Selected To Demonstrate Advanced Capabilities For U.S. Space Force’s Space Based Interceptor Program | Rocket Lab
item.description: LONG BEACH, Calif., May 7, 2026 – Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB), a leading launch and space systems company, today announced that in partnership with Raytheon it has been selected to demonstrate advanced capabilities for the United States Space Force’s Space Based Interceptor program. The…

item.title: Rocket Lab Unveils New High-Performance Star Tracker Optimized for Accuracy in Increased Radiation Environments | Rocket Lab
item.description: Long Beach, California. April 23, 2026: Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB) (“Rocket Lab” or “the Company”), a global leader in launch services and space systems, today announced its next-generation High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP), designed to improve key performance metrics for longer…

item.title: March 2026 Company Update
item.description: March brought continued progress across the Terran R program, with key milestones across design, manufacturing, testing, and launch preparations.  Design This month, the team released 2,207 flight parts, with a continued focus on completing the stage one shipping assembly. Work remains c

item.title: February 2026 Company Update
item.description: February marked another month of critical progress across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, the team released an additional 1,826 flight components. The stage two shipping assembly and stage two fluids top-level assembly are now fully released, enabling final integration activit

item.title: January 2026 Company Update
item.description: January marked a strong start to the year, with meaningful progress across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, the team released 1,856 flight parts. At this stage of the program, part releases are primarily for secondary structures and integration hardware that will be installed l

item.title: December 2025 Company Update
item.description: As we start the new year, here’s a look at key Terran R program milestones from December 2025.  Design This month, the team released 1,526 flight parts and the integrated thrust structure fluids assembly, unlocking downstream manufacturing and keeping the integration schedule moving at p
